name: Tárrega - Bellpuig - Sant Martí de Malda - Vallbona de les Monges - Verdú — circular through the plains of Lérida
name_original: Tárrega - Bellpuig - Sant Martí de Malda - Vallbona de les Monges - Verdú — circular por los llanos de Lérida
region_intro: Touring cycling around Tàrrega is characterized by its expansive, fertile agricultural lands and gently undulating terrain within the Urgell plain. The region features a network of historical waterways, notably the Canal d'Urgell, which provides unique cycling experiences. Routes often follow flat zones carved by rivers like the Sió, offering scenic rides through olive groves, vineyards, and orchards. This landscape makes Tàrrega suitable for various cycling disciplines and skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Tàrrega The most popular touring cycling route is Tárrega - Bellpuig - Sant Martí de Malda - Vallbona de les Monges - Verdú — circular through the plains of Lérida, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses agricultural plains and connects historical villages, including the Monastery of Santa María de Vallbona. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Camino Real through Fonolleres – Sant Pere el Gros Church loop from Tàrrega, a moderate 15.1 miles (24.4 km) path. This route explores the historic Camino Real and passes by the Sant Pere el Gros Church. Local touring cyclists also love the Historic Center of Tàrrega – Sant Eloi Park of Tàrrega loop from Tàrrega, a 31.4 miles (50.5 km) trail leading through the historic center and local park, often completed in about 4 hours 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Tàrrega is defined by agricultural plains, historical waterways like the Canal d'Urgell, and gentle inclines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy leisure rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Tàrrega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tàrrega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling around Tàrrega?
A: The terrain around Tàrrega is primarily characterized by the expansive, fertile &lt;strong&gt;Urgell plain&lt;/strong&gt;. Cyclists can expect mostly flat to gently undulating landscapes, making it suitable for leisure rides. Routes often wind through agricultural lands, including olive groves, vineyards, and orchards, with some following historical waterways like the Canal d'Urgell and the Sió river.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Tàrrega area?
A: There are over 65 touring cycling routes available around Tàrrega. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy, moderate, to difficult.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Tàrrega?
A: Yes, Tàrrega offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tàrrega – Camino Real through Fonolleres loop from Tàrrega&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 8.5-mile (13.7 km) path with minimal elevation gain, offering a pleasant ride through the local landscape.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore while cycling around Tàrrega?
A: The region is rich in historical and cultural sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Vallbona&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th-century Cistercian abbey. Another notable landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de Almenara Tower&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th-12th century defensive tower offering panoramic views of the Urgell plain. The historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera&lt;/a&gt; is also within cycling reach.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Tàrrega?
A: Yes, many routes in Tàrrega are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968973869/tarrega-bellpuig-sant-marti-de-malda-vallbona-de-les-monges-verdu-circular-through-the-plains-of-lerida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tárrega - Bellpuig - Sant Martí de Malda - Vallbona de les Monges - Verdú — circular through the plains of Lérida&lt;/a&gt; is a 31.6-mile (50.9 km) loop that connects several historical villages.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Tàrrega?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tàrrega are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the peaceful rides through expansive agricultural plains, the historical waterways like the Canal d'Urgell, and the opportunity to visit charming villages and cultural sites along the way.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tàrrega?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially in the open plains, while winters are generally mild but can be cooler.

Q: Are there routes that follow the historic Canal d'Urgell?
A: Yes, the Canal d'Urgell is a central feature of the region's cycling landscape. Routes like the 'Pedals del Canal d'Urgell' offer a unique 'slow cycling' experience along its historic banks, providing gentle inclines and picturesque scenery through green fields and rural areas. These routes are often suitable for gravel bikes, hardtail MTBs, and e-bikes.

Q: Are there options for parking near the cycling routes in Tàrrega?
A: Tàrrega, being a regional hub, offers various parking options within the town itself. For specific trailheads outside the town, it's advisable to check local signage or use navigation apps for designated parking areas, especially in smaller villages or near popular attractions.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the 'Route des Châteaux du Sió'?
A: Yes, the region offers routes that traverse the flat area defined by the Sió river, which is part of the 'La Route des Châteaux du Sió'. These routes provide a journey through historical sites, including various castles, and offer scenic rides through agricultural fields. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14946840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tàrrega – Guimerà Historic Village loop from Tàrrega&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through historic areas.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Tàrrega?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Tàrrega offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tàrrega – Sant Eloi Park of Tàrrega loop from Tàrrega&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 31.4-mile (50.5 km) trail with significant elevation gain, often taking nearly 5 hours to complete, providing a rewarding workout.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay along the cycling routes in Tàrrega?
A: Tàrrega and the surrounding villages offer various amenities for cyclists. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in towns like Tàrrega, Bellpuig, and Verdú. The 'Pedals del Canal d'Urgell' route, for instance, is known to have amenities and accommodation available along its length, catering to touring cyclists.
tips: We are at the Castle of Bellpuig, a place with charm and history that we must visit along with the medieval and Renaissance surroundings of the town of Bellpuig, located in the Urgell region. Apart from the castle, we can visit the parish church of Sant Nicolau from the 16th century.
Religious building, 16th-17th century. Baroque style and located in the main square of the town of Sant Martí de Maldà. It is a Cultural Asset of National Interest (BCIN).

The monastery of Santa María de Vallbona is a female Cistercian abbey founded in the 11th century and built in a mixture of Romanesque and Gothic styles. Together with the Poblet and Santes Creus monasteries, it forms the Cistercian Route.

Near Vallbona de les Monges, in Lérida, we find these so-called "scarecrows" that are there for the exact opposite, since it is part of a project called ''Estima Ocells'' (he wants birds) and on their heads, there are birdhouses, and thus, attract them. It is one of the most photographed places by bicycle lovers, in the province of Lérida.

The castle of Verdú is located in the heart of the town next to one of the most beautiful and unique churches in Catalonia, the church of Santa María de Verdú. The castle preserves different architectural elements: Romanesque, Gothic and Renaissance. We will find information panels, but I recommend hiring the guided tour at the Verdú tourist office.
